entries.title: NASA confirms this incredible fact: The Moon Is Drifting Away
entries.hook: NASA confirms this incredible fact:
stories: The Moon moves 3.8 cm away from Earth every year. When it formed 4.5 billion years ago it was 20 times closer. In 50 billion years a day on Earth will last 1000 hours.
Uranus is tilted 98 degrees essentially rolling around the Sun on its side. A massive collision with an Earth-sized object likely knocked it over 4 billion years ago.
Without the Moon days would become 12 hours long. Earth's tilt could shift to 45° causing extreme seasons and mass extinction. Tides would shrink by 75%.

entries.hook: The cosmos has secrets we're just discovering:
space_facts: The Sun Is 99.86% of All Mass
The Solar System Has a Tail
stories: The Sun contains 99.86% of all mass in our solar system. All planets moons asteroids and comets combined make up only 0.14%. 1.3 million Earths could fit inside the Sun.
A storm larger than Earth has raged on Jupiter for at least 400 years. It is 16000 km wide with winds reaching 640 km/h. Scientists don't know why it hasn't dissipated.
The solar system moves through space at 828000 km/h with a comet-like tail called the heliotail made of charged particles. It extends trillions of kilometers.

entries.title: Space is weirder than science fiction: Venus Day Longer Than Year
entries.hook: Space is weirder than science fiction:
stories: Venus takes 225 Earth days to orbit the Sun but 243 days to rotate. A day is longer than a year. Venus also rotates backward so the Sun rises in the west.
The Moon moves 3.8 cm away from Earth every year. When it formed 4.5 billion years ago it was 20 times closer. In 50 billion years a day on Earth will last 1000 hours.
Saturn's north pole has a hexagonal cloud pattern 32000 km across each side longer than Earth's diameter. First seen by Voyager in 1981 no other planet has anything like it.

entries.title: The universe is bigger than you can imagine: More Stars Than Sand
entries.hook: The universe is bigger than you can imagine:
stories: There are about 100 billion galaxies each with 100 billion stars. That is 10 sextillion stars more than all grains of sand on every beach on Earth.
Mercury rotates once every 59 Earth days but orbits the Sun in 88 days. From sunrise to sunrise a full day-night cycle on Mercury takes 176 Earth days.
Mars has Olympus Mons the largest volcano in the solar system at 21.9 km tall 2.5 times Everest. Its base is the size of Arizona. From the peak you wouldn't see the edge.

entries.title: This space fact will blow your mind: Neutron Stars Are Insanely Dense
entries.hook: This space fact will blow your mind:
space_facts: The Solar System Has a Tail
stories: A neutron star is 20 km across but contains more mass than the Sun. One teaspoon weighs 10 million tons the same as every human on Earth combined.
Uranus is tilted 98 degrees essentially rolling around the Sun on its side. A massive collision with an Earth-sized object likely knocked it over 4 billion years ago.
The solar system moves through space at 828000 km/h with a comet-like tail called the heliotail made of charged particles. It extends trillions of kilometers.

entries.title: The cosmos has secrets we're just discovering: More Stars Than Sand
entries.hook: The cosmos has secrets we're just discovering:
stories: There are about 100 billion galaxies each with 100 billion stars. That is 10 sextillion stars more than all grains of sand on every beach on Earth.
When the Moon formed 4.5 billion years ago Earth rotated much faster. A day was only 6 hours long. The Moon's gravity has been slowing Earth's rotation for billions of years.
Saturn's north pole has a hexagonal cloud pattern 32000 km across each side longer than Earth's diameter. First seen by Voyager in 1981 no other planet has anything like it.

entries.title: The cosmos has secrets we're just discovering: Neutron Stars Are Insanely Dense
entries.hook: The cosmos has secrets we're just discovering:
stories: A neutron star is 20 km across but contains more mass than the Sun. One teaspoon weighs 10 million tons the same as every human on Earth combined.
The white dwarf star BPM 37093 nicknamed Lucy is a giant crystallized carbon sphere 4000 km across. It weighs 10 billion trillion trillion carats the largest diamond ever found.
A black hole the size of a golf ball would contain the mass of Mount Everest. A tennis ball-sized one would have the mass of Earth.

entries.title: NASA confirms this incredible fact: Venus Day Longer Than Year

space_facts: The Solar System Has a Tail
stories: Venus takes 225 Earth days to orbit the Sun but 243 days to rotate. A day is longer than a year. Venus also rotates backward so the Sun rises in the west.
A neutron star is 20 km across but contains more mass than the Sun. One teaspoon weighs 10 million tons the same as every human on Earth combined.
The solar system moves through space at 828000 km/h with a comet-like tail called the heliotail made of charged particles. It extends trillions of kilometers.

space_facts: The Sun Is 99.86% of All Mass
stories: Venus takes 225 Earth days to orbit the Sun but 243 days to rotate. A day is longer than a year. Venus also rotates backward so the Sun rises in the west.
The Sun contains 99.86% of all mass in our solar system. All planets moons asteroids and comets combined make up only 0.14%. 1.3 million Earths could fit inside the Sun.
Saturn's north pole has a hexagonal cloud pattern 32000 km across each side longer than Earth's diameter. First seen by Voyager in 1981 no other planet has anything like it.

entries.title: The cosmos has secrets we're just discovering: Giant Diamond in Space
entries.hook: The cosmos has secrets we're just discovering:
stories: The white dwarf star BPM 37093 nicknamed Lucy is a giant crystallized carbon sphere 4000 km across. It weighs 10 billion trillion trillion carats the largest diamond ever found.
In 2003 MIT scientists created a Bose-Einstein condensate at 0.5 nanokelvin half a billionth above absolute zero. That is colder than the Boomerang Nebula at 1 Kelvin.
Without the Moon days would become 12 hours long. Earth's tilt could shift to 45° causing extreme seasons and mass extinction. Tides would shrink by 75%.

entries.title: The universe is bigger than you can imagine: Uranus Rolls Sideways
entries.hook: The universe is bigger than you can imagine:
stories: Uranus is tilted 98 degrees essentially rolling around the Sun on its side. A massive collision with an Earth-sized object likely knocked it over 4 billion years ago.
Mars has Olympus Mons the largest volcano in the solar system at 21.9 km tall 2.5 times Everest. Its base is the size of Arizona. From the peak you wouldn't see the edge.
A black hole the size of a golf ball would contain the mass of Mount Everest. A tennis ball-sized one would have the mass of Earth.

entries.title: The universe is bigger than you can imagine: Neutron Stars Are Insanely Dense
entries.hook: The universe is bigger than you can imagine:
stories: A neutron star is 20 km across but contains more mass than the Sun. One teaspoon weighs 10 million tons the same as every human on Earth combined.
When the Moon formed 4.5 billion years ago Earth rotated much faster. A day was only 6 hours long. The Moon's gravity has been slowing Earth's rotation for billions of years.
Saturn's north pole has a hexagonal cloud pattern 32000 km across each side longer than Earth's diameter. First seen by Voyager in 1981 no other planet has anything like it.

entries.title: The universe is bigger than you can imagine: The Moon Is Drifting Away
entries.hook: The universe is bigger than you can imagine:
space_facts: The Sun Is 99.86% of All Mass
stories: The Moon moves 3.8 cm away from Earth every year. When it formed 4.5 billion years ago it was 20 times closer. In 50 billion years a day on Earth will last 1000 hours.
The Sun contains 99.86% of all mass in our solar system. All planets moons asteroids and comets combined make up only 0.14%. 1.3 million Earths could fit inside the Sun.
A storm larger than Earth has raged on Jupiter for at least 400 years. It is 16000 km wide with winds reaching 640 km/h. Scientists don't know why it hasn't dissipated.

entries.title: Space is weirder than science fiction: More Stars Than Sand
entries.hook: Space is weirder than science fiction:
stories: There are about 100 billion galaxies each with 100 billion stars. That is 10 sextillion stars more than all grains of sand on every beach on Earth.
Mercury rotates once every 59 Earth days but orbits the Sun in 88 days. From sunrise to sunrise a full day-night cycle on Mercury takes 176 Earth days.
Uranus is tilted 98 degrees essentially rolling around the Sun on its side. A massive collision with an Earth-sized object likely knocked it over 4 billion years ago.
